mechanism_narrative: Declining payroll employment (PAYEMS) signals weakening labor demand and future earnings pressure, causing equity investors to reduce growth expectations and sell equities, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 131516.0 thousand as of October 2011
regime_hint: post-shock recovery with fragile labor market
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of months where PAYEMS declines month-over-month, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate at near-zero levels (FEDFUNDS = 0.07%) indicates an exhausted monetary easing cycle with limited further stimulus capacity. When combined with persistent inflation (CPIAUCSL), this regime constrains real returns and produces negative equity forward returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.07% and CPIAUCSL at 226.955 as of September-October 2011
regime_hint: zero lower bound monetary constraint with inflation persistence
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are positive in > 60% of triggered instances, or the interaction term (FEDFUNDS × CPIAUCSL) shows Rank IC < 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) directly reflect forward residential investment and consumer confidence. A sustained decline in HOUST signals weakening aggregate demand and future GDP contraction, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days as forward-looking equity markets reprice growth expectations.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 658.0 thousand units as of September 2011
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns are positive in > 65% of months where HOUST declines > 5%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) measures real economic capacity utilization and manufacturing health. Rising INDPRO signals strengthening supply-side capacity and profit margins, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as earnings expectations improve.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 94.1851 index points as of September 2011
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are negative in > 55% of months where INDPRO rises month-over-month, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) jointly capture consumer and residential demand. When both decline simultaneously, this signals a synchronized demand shock across consumption and investment, producing sharply negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as equity risk premiums widen.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS month-over-month change < -0.5% AND HOUST month-over-month change < -3%
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 397666.0 million dollars and HOUST at 658.0 thousand units as of October 2011
regime_hint: synchronized demand contraction in late-cycle downturn
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are positive in > 60% of jointly-triggered instances, or the interaction term shows Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The unemployment rate (UNRATE) at 9.0% signals persistent slack in labor markets. When UNRATE rises above 8.5%, it indicates accelerating job losses and declining consumer purchasing power, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as earnings forecasts are revised downward.
regime_hint: post-recession labor market recovery with elevated slack
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are positive in > 65% of months where UNRATE > 8.5%, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real interest rates (approximated by FEDFUNDS minus CPIAUCSL inflation trend) turn deeply negative when policy rates are near-zero and inflation persists. This regime erodes fixed-income returns and forces equity risk-seeking, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days as portfolio rebalancing accelerates.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.07% and CPIAUCSL at 226.955 as of September-October 2011
regime_hint: financial repression regime with negative real rates
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns are negative in > 55% of triggered instances, or the interaction term shows Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP (GDPC1) growth decelerates when demand weakens. A quarter-over-quarter GDP growth rate below 1.5% annualized signals late-cycle stagnation and recession risk, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as equity valuations compress on lower growth expectations.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 13352.8 billion chained 2017 dollars as of Q2 2011
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with decelerating growth momentum
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are positive in > 60% of quarters where GDPC1 growth < 1.5%, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.
